steps.title: Identify each input and output
steps.content: The problem says $x$ is the depth below the surface in millimeters, and $f(x)$ is the predicted intensity in photons. So $f(11)=300$ describes the intensity when the depth is $11$ millimeters.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
Substituting $x=11$ gives the same output stated in the question.
$$f(11)=600(0.5)^{\frac{11}{11}}=600(0.5)=300$$
So the statement means a beam $11$ millimeters below the surface has a predicted intensity of $300$ photons. The answer is C.
